acceptedAnswer.text: No. Copyright exists automatically upon creation. But registration provides evidentiary advantage in infringement disputes and is recommended for commercial works.

mainEntity.name: How long does copyright registration take?

acceptedAnswer.text: 2-6 months. 30-day mandatory waiting period for objections. Processing time depends on Copyright Office workload.
Government fee: \u20b9500 per work. Professional fee depends on type and complexity. Total from \u20b95,999.

Literary works (books, articles, code), artistic works (paintings, photos, logos), musical works, sound recordings, cinematograph films, and computer programs. Ideas cannot be copyrighted — only expression of ideas.
Author lifetime + 60 years (for literary, dramatic, musical, artistic works). 60 years from publication for anonymous works, photographs, cinematograph films, sound recordings, and government works.

mainEntity.name: Is registration mandatory for copyright protection?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Copyright exists automatically upon creation. But registration provides: legal evidence of ownership, right to claim statutory damages, ability to file infringement suits, and presumption of validity in court.
Yes. Computer programs are protected as literary works under Copyright Act, 1957. Source code and object code both protectable. Registration recommended for commercial software.
